+# Helper classes
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+class Empty:
+    """Empty class."""
+    def __init__(self):
+        """TODO: docstring in public method."""
+        return
+
+    def __nonzero__(self):
+        """Empty is falsy."""
+        return False
+
+    def __bool__(self):
+        """Empty is falsy (Python 3)."""
+        return False
+
+    def __repr__(self):
+        """Empty repr."""
+        return "Empty"
+
+
+ReturnedNone = Empty()
+
+
+class ExceptionHolder:
+    """Basic exception handler."""
+    def __init__(self, node, exc=None, msg='', expr=None, lineno=None):
+        """TODO: docstring in public method."""
+        self.node = node
+        self.expr = expr
+        self.msg = msg
+        self.exc = exc
+        self.lineno = lineno
+        self.exc_info = exc_info()
+        if self.exc is None and self.exc_info[0] is not None:
+            self.exc = self.exc_info[0]
+        if self.msg == '' and self.exc_info[1] is not None:
+            self.msg = self.exc_info[1]
+
+    def get_error(self):
+        """Retrieve error data.
+        Return a tuple of the exception name and the error message.
+        The exception name is the name of the exception class.
+        The error message is the error message of the exception.
+        """
+        if self.exc is not None:
+            exc_name = self.exc.__name__ if isinstance(self.exc, type) else type(self.exc).__name__
+        elif self.exc_info[0] is not None:
+            exc_name = self.exc_info[0].__name__
+        else:
+            exc_name = ''
+        return (exc_name, str(self.msg))
+
+
+class Group(dict):
+    """
+    Group: a container of objects that can be accessed either as an object attributes
+    or dictionary  key/value.  Attribute names must follow Python naming conventions.
+    """
+    def __init__(self, name=None, searchgroups=None, **kws):
+        if name is None:
+            name = hex(id(self))
+        self.__name__ = name
+        dict.__init__(self, **kws)
+        self._searchgroups = searchgroups
+
+    def __setattr__(self, name, value):
+        """Set an attribute."""
+        if name == '_searchgroups' or name == '__name__':
+            dict.__setattr__(self, name, value)
+        else:
+            self[name] = value
+
+    def __getattr__(self, name, default=None):
+        """Get an attribute.
+        If the attribute is not found, return the default value.
+        If the attribute is found, return the value.
+        If the attribute is not found and no default value is provided, raise a KeyError.
+        """
+        try:
+            return self[name]
+        except KeyError:
+            if default is not None:
+                return default
+            raise
+
+    def __setitem__(self, name, value):
+        """Set an item."""
+        dict.__setitem__(self, name, value)
+
+    def get(self, key, default=None):
+        """Get an item.
+        If the item is not found, return the default value.
+        If the item is found, return the value.
+        If the item is not found and no default value is provided, raise a KeyError.
+        """
+        try:
+            return self[key]
+        except KeyError:
+            if default is not None:
+                return default
+            raise
+
+    def __repr__(self):
+        """Representation of the Group object.
+        Return a string representation of the Group object.
+        The string representation is a list of the keys in the Group object.
+        """
+        return f"Group({self.__name__}, {list(self.keys())})"
+
+    def _repr_html_(self):
+        """HTML representation for Jupyter notebook"""
+        html = [f"<table><caption>Group('{self.__name__}')</caption>",
+                "<tr><th>Attribute</th><th>DataType</th><th><b>Value</b></th></tr>"]
+        for key, val in self.items():
+            html.append(f"""
+<tr><td>{key}</td><td><i>{type(val).__name__}</i></td>
+    <td>{repr(val):.75s}</td>
+</tr>""")
+        html.append("</table>")
+        return '\n'.join(html)
+
+
+class Procedure:
+    """Procedure: user-defined function for asteval.
+
+    This stores the parsed ast nodes as from the 'functiondef' ast node
+    for later evaluation.
+
+    """
+
+    def __init__(self, name, interp, doc=None, lineno=0,
+                 body=None, args=None, kwargs=None,
+                 vararg=None, varkws=None):
+        """TODO: docstring in public method."""
+        self.__ininit__ = True
+        self.name = name
+        self.__name__ = self.name
+        self.__asteval__ = interp
+        self.raise_exc = self.__asteval__.raise_exception
+        self.__doc__ = doc
+        self.body = body
+        self.argnames = args
+        self.kwargs = kwargs
+        self.vararg = vararg
+        self.varkws = varkws
+        self.lineno = lineno
+        self.__ininit__ = False
+
+    def __setattr__(self, attr, val):
+        """Set an attribute."""
+        if not getattr(self, '__ininit__', False):
+            if attr == '__asteval__':
+                dict.__setattr__(self, attr, val)
+                return
+            if attr in ('body', 'argnames', 'kwargs', 'vararg', 'varkws',
+                        'lineno', 'name', '__name__', '__doc__',
+                        'raise_exc', '__ininit__', 'symtable', '_symtable'):
+                dict.__setattr__(self, attr, val)
+                return
+            # User-defined attributes go into the symbol table of the
+            # interpreter
+            self.__asteval__.symtable[attr] = val
+        else:
+            dict.__setattr__(self, attr, val)
+
+    def __dir__(self):
+        return ['name']
+
+    def __repr__(self):
+        """TODO: docstring in magic method.
+        Return a string representation of the Procedure object.
+        The string representation is a list of the keys in the Procedure object.
+        """
+        return f"<asteval Procedure '{self.name}'>"
+
+    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
+        """TODO: docstring in public method.
+        Call the Procedure object.
+        The Procedure object is called with the given arguments and keyword arguments.
+        """
+        # Build a temporary symbol table for the function call
+        # This is handled by the Interpreter's on_call method
+        interp = self.__asteval__
+        return interp._run_procedure(self, *args, **kwargs)
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Symbol table builder
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def make_symbol_table(use_numpy=True, nested=False, top=True, **kws):
+    """Create a default symbol table with optional NumPy support and custom symbols.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    use_numpy : bool, optional
+        Whether to include symbols from NumPy, default is True
+    nested : bool, optional
+        Whether to create a nested symbol table instead of a plain dict, default is False
+    top : bool, optional
+        Whether this is the top-level table in a nested table, default is True
+    **kws : dict
+        Dictionary of user-defined symbols to add to the symbol table
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    dict
+        A symbol table dictionary with built-in functions and optional user symbols
+    """
+    out = dict(BUILTINS_TABLE)
+    out.update(MATH_TABLE)
+
+    if HAS_NUMPY and use_numpy:
+        for n in FROM_NUMPY:
+            if hasattr(numpy, n):
+                out[n] = getattr(numpy, n)
+        # Handle numpy renames
+        for alias, realname in NUMPY_RENAMES.items():
+            if realname in out:
+                out[alias] = out[realname]
+
+        if HAS_NUMPY_FINANCIAL:
+            for n in FROM_NUMPY_FINANCIAL:
+                if hasattr(numpy_financial, n):
+                    out[n] = getattr(numpy_financial, n)
+
+    # Add local safe functions
+    out.update(LOCALFUNCS)
+
+    # Add user-defined symbols
+    for key, val in kws.items():
+        out[key] = val
+
+    if nested and top:
+        out = Group(name='top', **out)
+
+    return out
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Module export list
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+__all__ = [
+    'Interpreter', 'NameFinder', 'valid_symbol_name',
+    'make_symbol_table', 'get_ast_names', '__version__',
+    'Empty', 'ExceptionHolder', 'Group', 'Procedure',
+    'HAS_NUMPY', 'HAS_NUMPY_FINANCIAL', 'MAX_EXPONENT', 'MAX_STR_LEN',
+    'MAX_SHIFT', 'MAX_OPEN_BUFFER', 'RESERVED_WORDS', 'NAME_MATCH',
+    'UNSAFE_ATTRS', 'FROM_PY', 'BUILTINS_TABLE', 'FROM_MATH', 'MATH_TABLE',
+    'FROM_NUMPY', 'FROM_NUMPY_FINANCIAL', 'NUMPY_RENAMES',
+    'LOCALFUNCS', 'OPERATORS', 'ReturnedNone',
+    'op2func', 'valid_varname', '_open', '_type',
+    'safe_pow', 'safe_mult', 'safe_add', 'safe_lshift',
+]

@@ -0,0 +1,63 @@
+[build-system]
+requires = ["setuptools>=61.0", "wheel"]
+build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
+
+[project]
+name = "asteval"
+version = "1.0.0"
+description = "A safe expression evaluator Python library designed to parse and execute Python expressions in a restricted and secure manner."
+readme = "README.rst"
+license = {file = "LICENSE"}
+requires-python = ">=3.11"
+authors = [
+    {name = "The asteval contributors"}
+]
+keywords = ["asteval", "safe-eval", "ast", "expression-evaluator"]
+classifiers = [
+    "Development Status :: 5 - Production/Stable",
+    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
+    "License :: OSI Approved :: BSD License",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
+    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
+    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules",
+]
+dependencies = [
+    "numpy>=1.20.0",
+]
+
+[project.optional-dependencies]
+test = [
+    "numpy>=1.20.0",
+    "numpy_financial>=1.0.0",
+    "pytest>=6.0.0",
+    "pytest-cov>=2.0.0",
+    "coverage>=5.0.0",
+]
+dev = [
+    "numpy>=1.20.0",
+    "numpy_financial>=1.0.0",
+    "pytest>=6.0.0",
+    "pytest-cov>=2.0.0",
+    "coverage>=5.0.0",
+]
+
+[project.urls]
+Homepage = "https://github.com/newville/asteval"
+Documentation = "https://asteval.readthedocs.io/"
+
+[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
+include = ["asteval*"]
+
+[tool.pytest.ini_options]
+testpaths = ["tests"]
+python_files = ["test_*.py"]
+python_classes = ["Test*"]
+python_functions = ["test_*"]
+
+[tool.coverage.run]
+source = ["asteval"]
+branch = true
+
+[tool.coverage.report]
+omit = ["tests/*"]
+show_missing = true

+"""
+Comprehensive test suite for asteval - safe expression evaluator.
+Covers: basic expressions, dictionary indexing, list slicing, tuple/set operations,
+string operations, f-strings, numpy arrays, loops, conditionals, functions,
+comprehensions, exceptions, math, symbol tables, safety, AST parsing, config, errors, I/O.
+"""
+import pytest
+import ast
+import sys
+import time
+import tempfile
+import os
+from io import StringIO
+
+from asteval import Interpreter, NameFinder, make_symbol_table, get_ast_names, valid_symbol_name
+from asteval.astutils import Empty, ExceptionHolder, Group, Procedure, valid_varname, HAS_NUMPY, HAS_NUMPY_FINANCIAL
+
+
+# ============================================================
+# Node 1: Basic Expression Evaluation
+# ============================================================
+class TestBasicExpressions:
+    def test_integer_literal(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("4") == 4
+
+    def test_float_literal(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("3.14") == 3.14
+
+    def test_string_literal(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("'x'") == 'x'
+
+    def test_bytes_literal(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("b'x'") == b'x'
+
+    def test_str_conversion(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("str(4)") == '4'
+
+    def test_repr_conversion(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("repr(4)") == '4'
+
+    def test_ellipsis(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("...") is Ellipsis
+
+    def test_false(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("False") is False
+
+    def test_true(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("True") is True
+
+    def test_none(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("None") is None
+
+    def test_variable_assignment_and_access(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("x = 8")
+        assert interp("x") == 8
+
+
+# ============================================================
+# Node 2: Dictionary Indexing
+# ============================================================
+class TestDictionaryIndexing:
+    def test_dict_creation_and_access(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("a_dict = {'a': 1, 'b': 2, 'c': 3, 'd': 4}")
+        assert interp("a_dict['a']") == 1
+        assert interp("a_dict['c']") == 3
+
+    def test_dict_element_modification(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("a_dict = {'a': 1, 'b': 2}")
+        interp("a_dict['a'] = 10")
+        assert interp("a_dict['a']") == 10
+
+    def test_nested_dict(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("nested_dict = {'outer': {'inner': 42}}")
+        assert interp("nested_dict['outer']['inner']") == 42
+
+    def test_empty_dict(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("d = {}")
+        assert interp("d") == {}
+
+    def test_dict_len(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("d = {'a': 1, 'b': 2, 'c': 3}")
+        assert interp("len(d)") == 3
+
+
+# ============================================================
+# Node 3: List Indexing and Slicing
+# ============================================================
+class TestListIndexing:
+    def test_list_creation_and_access(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("xlist = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]")
+        assert interp("xlist[0]") == 1
+        assert interp("xlist[2]") == 3
+
+    def test_list_slicing(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("xlist = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]")
+        assert interp("xlist[1:3]") == [2, 3]
+        assert interp("xlist[::2]") == [1, 3, 5]
+
+    def test_list_element_modification(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("xlist = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]")
+        interp("xlist[0] = 10")
+        assert interp("xlist[0]") == 10
+
+    def test_list_append(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("xlist = [1, 2, 3]")
+        interp("xlist.append(4)")
+        assert interp("xlist") == [1, 2, 3, 4]
+
+    def test_negative_index(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("xlist = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]")
+        assert interp("xlist[-1]") == 5
+
+    def test_empty_list(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("xlist = []")
+        assert interp("xlist") == []
+
+
+# ============================================================
+# Node 4: Tuple and Set Operations
+# ============================================================
+class TestTupleSetOperations:
+    def test_tuple_creation_and_access(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("atuple = (1, 2, 3, 4)")
+        assert interp("atuple[0]") == 1
+
+    def test_tuple_slicing(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("atuple = (1, 2, 3, 4)")
+        assert interp("atuple[1:3]") == (2, 3)
+
+    def test_set_creation(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("aset = {1, 2, 3, 4}")
+        assert interp("len(aset)") == 4
+
+    def test_set_union(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("set1 = {1, 2, 3}")
+        interp("set2 = {3, 4, 5}")
+        assert interp("set1 | set2") == {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}
+
+    def test_set_intersection(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("set1 = {1, 2, 3}")
+        interp("set2 = {3, 4, 5}")
+        assert interp("set1 & set2") == {3}
+
+    def test_set_difference(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("set1 = {1, 2, 3}")
+        interp("set2 = {2, 3, 4}")
+        assert interp("set1 - set2") == {1}
+
+    def test_single_element_tuple(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("t = (42,)")
+        assert interp("t") == (42,)
+
+
+# ============================================================
+# Node 5: String Operations
+# ============================================================
+class TestStringOperations:
+    def test_string_creation_and_access(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("astring = 'hello world'")
+        assert interp("astring[0]") == 'h'
+
+    def test_string_slicing(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("astring = 'hello world'")
+        assert interp("astring[1:5]") == 'ello'
+
+    def test_string_upper(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("astring = 'hello world'")
+        assert interp("astring.upper()") == 'HELLO WORLD'
+
+    def test_string_split(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("astring = 'hello world'")
+        assert interp("astring.split()") == ['hello', 'world']
+
+    def test_string_concatenation(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("'hello' + ' ' + 'world'") == 'hello world'
+
+    def test_string_length(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("s = 'abc'")
+        assert interp("len(s)") == 3
+
+
+# ============================================================
+# Node 6: F-string Support
+# ============================================================
+class TestFStringSupport:
+    def test_basic_fstring_format(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("x = 2523.33/723")
+        interp("s = f'{x:+.3f}'")
+        assert interp("s") == '+3.490'
+
+    def test_fstring_string_conversion(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("chie = '\u03c7(E)'")
+        interp("v_s = f'{chie!s}'")
+        assert interp("v_s") == '\u03c7(E)'
+
+    def test_fstring_repr_conversion(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("chie = '\u03c7(E)'")
+        interp("v_r = f'{chie!r}'")
+        assert interp("v_r") == "'\u03c7(E)'"
+
+    def test_fstring_ascii_conversion(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("chie = '\u03c7(E)'")
+        interp("v_a = f'{chie!a}'")
+        # !a conversion: the actual Python result is "'\\u03c7(E)'"
+        assert interp("v_a") == "'\\u03c7(E)'"
+
+    def test_fstring_variable(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("name = 'world'")
+        interp("greeting = f'Hello, {name}!'")
+        assert interp("greeting") == 'Hello, world!'
+
+    def test_fstring_expression(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("x = 5")
+        interp("y = f'{x*2}'")
+        assert interp("y") == '10'
+
+
+# ============================================================
+# Node 7: NumPy Array Operations
+# ============================================================
+class TestNumpyArrayOperations:
+    @pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NUMPY, reason="numpy not installed")
+    def test_1d_array_index(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("a_ndarray = 5*arange(20)")
+        assert interp("a_ndarray[2]") == 10
+        assert interp("a_ndarray[4]") == 20
+
+    @pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NUMPY, reason="numpy not installed")
+    def test_2d_array_slicing(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("a_ndarray = arange(200).reshape(10, 20)")
+        result = interp("a_ndarray[1:3,5:7]")
+        assert result.tolist() == [[25, 26], [45, 46]]
+
+    @pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NUMPY, reason="numpy not installed")
+    def test_array_column_slice(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("y = arange(20).reshape(4, 5)")
+        result = interp("y[:,3]")
+        assert result.tolist() == [3, 8, 13, 18]
+
+    @pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NUMPY, reason="numpy not installed")
+    def test_array_ellipsis(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("y = arange(20).reshape(4, 5)")
+        result = interp("y[...,1]")
+        assert result.tolist() == [1, 6, 11, 16]

+    @pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NUMPY, reason="numpy not installed")

+class TestFunctionDefinition:
+    def test_basic_function(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("""
+def fcn(x, scale=2):
+    'test function'
+    out = sqrt(x)
+    if scale > 1:
+        out = out * scale
+    return out
+""")
+        assert interp("fcn(4, scale=9)") == 18
+        assert interp("fcn(9, scale=0)") == 3
+
+    def test_varargs_function(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("""
+def fcn(*args):
+    'test varargs function'
+    out = 0
+    for i in args:
+        out = out + i*i
+    return out
+""")
+        assert interp("fcn(1,2,3)") == 14
+
+    def test_kwargs_function(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("""
+def fcn(x=0, y=0, z=0, t=0, square=False):
+    'test kwargs function'
+    out = 0
+    for i in (x, y, z, t):
+        if square:
+            out = out + i*i
+        else:
+            out = out + i
+    return out
+""")
+        assert interp("fcn(x=1, y=2, z=3, square=False)") == 6
+        assert interp("fcn(x=1, y=2, z=3, square=True)") == 14
+

+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("""

+""")

+# ============================================================
+# Node 12: Exception Handling

+class TestExceptionHandling:
+    def test_basic_exception(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("""
+x = 5
+try:
+    x = x/0
+except ZeroDivisionError:
+    print('Error Seen!')
+    x = -999
+""")
+        assert interp("x") == -999
+
+    def test_try_except_else_finally(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("""
+def dotry(x, y):
+    out, ok, clean = 0, False, False
+    try:
+        out = x/y
+    except ZeroDivisionError:
+        out = -1
+    else:
+        ok = True
+    finally:
+        clean = True
+    return out, ok, clean
+""")
+        assert interp("dotry(1, 2.0)") == (0.5, True, True)
+        assert interp("dotry(1, 0.0)") == (-1, False, True)
+
+    def test_general_exception(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("""

+        assert interp("x") == 15

+# ============================================================
+# Node 14: Symbol Table Management
+# ============================================================
+class TestSymbolTable:
+    def test_custom_symbol_table(self):
+        import math
+        def cosd(x):
+            return math.cos(math.radians(x))
+        def sind(x):
+            return math.sin(math.radians(x))
+        def tand(x):
+            return math.tan(math.radians(x))
+
+        sym_table = make_symbol_table(cosd=cosd, sind=sind, tand=tand)
+        aeval = Interpreter(symtable=sym_table)
+
+        aeval("x1 = sind(30)")
+        aeval("x2 = cosd(30)")
+        aeval("x3 = tand(45)")
+
+        assert abs(aeval("x1") - 0.5) < 0.001
+        assert abs(aeval("x2") - 0.866025) < 0.001
+        assert abs(aeval("x3") - 1.0) < 0.001
+
+    def test_readonly_symbols(self):
+        usersyms = {"a": 10, "b": 11, "c": 12, "d": 13, "x": 5, "y": 7}
+        aeval = Interpreter(user_symbols=usersyms, readonly_symbols={"a", "b", "c", "d"})
+
+        aeval("a = 20")  # should NOT change 'a'
+        aeval("x = 21")  # should change 'x'
+        aeval("y += a")
+
+        assert aeval("a") == 10  # unchanged
+        assert aeval("x") == 21  # changed
+        assert aeval("y") == 17  # 7 + 10
+
+    def test_user_defined_symbols(self):
+        aeval = Interpreter()
+        aeval("x = 1.1")
+        aeval("y = 2.5")
+        aeval("z = 788")
+
+        usersyms = aeval.user_defined_symbols()
+        assert 'x' in usersyms
+        assert 'y' in usersyms
+        assert 'z' in usersyms
+
+    def test_builtins_readonly(self):
+        aeval = Interpreter(builtins_readonly=True, minimal=True)
+        aeval("a_dict = {'a': 1, 'b': 2, 'c': 3, 'd': 4}")
+        assert aeval("a_dict['a'] == 1")
+        assert aeval("a_dict['c'] == 3")
+

+# ============================================================
+# Node 15: Safety Restrictions and Boundary Checks
+# ============================================================
+class TestSafetyRestrictions:
+    def test_statement_length_limit(self):
+        interp = Interpreter(max_statement_length=100)
+        longstr = "x = " + "1 + " * 100 + "1"
+        # When expression exceeds max, error is recorded but not raised by default
+        interp(longstr)
+        assert len(interp.error) > 0
+        errtype, errmsg = interp.error[0].get_error()
+        assert errtype == 'RuntimeError'
+        assert 'max_statement_length' in errmsg
+
+    def test_bit_shift_limit(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        assert interp("1<<1000") == (1 << 1000)
+        interp("1<<1001")
+        assert len(interp.error) > 0
+
+    def test_recursion_limit(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("def foo(): return foo()\nfoo()")
+        assert len(interp.error) > 0
+        err = interp.error[-1]
+        errtype, errmsg = err.get_error()
+        assert errtype == 'RecursionError'
+
+    def test_compile_disabled(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("compile('xxx')")
+        assert len(interp.error) > 0
+
+    def test_file_write_restricted(self):

+

+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp('open("nonexistent_file_xyz", "rb")')

+
+    def test_pow_safe(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("1.01**10000")
+        # Should not error for exponent at limit
+
+    def test_import_disabled_by_default(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("import os")
+        assert len(interp.error) > 0

+
+        interp.set_nodehandler('ifexp', handler)

+# ============================================================
+# Node 20: Performance Optimization and Resource Management
+# ============================================================
+class TestPerformance:
+    def test_execution_time(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        start_time = time.time()
+        interp("""
+sum_val = 0
+for i in range(1000):
+    sum_val += i
+""")
+        end_time = time.time()
+        execution_time = end_time - start_time
+        assert execution_time < 1.0
+        assert interp("sum_val") == 499500
+
+    def test_max_statement_length(self):
+        interp = Interpreter(max_statement_length=1000)
+        long_statement = "x = " + "1 + " * 1000 + "1"
+        # Error is recorded when exceeding length limit
+        interp(long_statement)
+        assert len(interp.error) > 0
+        errtype, errmsg = interp.error[0].get_error()
+        assert errtype == 'RuntimeError'
+
+    def test_recursion_depth(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("""
+def recursive_func(n):
+    if n <= 0:
+        return 0
+    return 1 + recursive_func(n-1)
+""")
+        result = interp("recursive_func(50)")
+        assert result == 50
+
+    def test_with_statement_cleanup(self):
+        temp_file = tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile('w', delete=False)
+        temp_file.write("test data")
+        temp_file.close()
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        interp("""
+with open('{}', 'r') as f:
+    data = f.read()
+""".format(temp_file.name))
+        assert interp("data") == "test data"
+        os.unlink(temp_file.name)
+
+    def test_safe_mult_string_limit(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        result = interp("'a' * 100")
+        assert len(result) == 100
+
+    def test_safe_add_string_limit(self):
+        interp = Interpreter()
+        result = interp("'a' + 'b'")
+        assert result == 'ab'
+
+
+# ============================================================
+# Additional Tests: Helper Classes and Functions
+# ============================================================
+class TestHelperClasses:
+    def test_empty_class(self):
+        e = Empty()
+        assert repr(e) == "Empty"
+        assert not e
+
+    def test_group_class(self):
+        g = Group(name="test", x=1, y=2)
+        assert g['x'] == 1
+        assert g.x == 1
+        assert g['y'] == 2
+        assert g.y == 2
+        g['z'] = 3
+        assert g.z == 3
+        g.w = 4
+        assert g['w'] == 4
+        assert g.get('x') == 1
+
+    def test_group_repr(self):
+        g = Group(name="test", x=1)
+        assert 'test' in g._repr_html_()
+
+    def test_valid_symbol_name(self):
+        assert valid_symbol_name('x') is True
+        assert valid_symbol_name('_private') is True
+        assert valid_symbol_name('class') is False
+        assert valid_symbol_name('123abc') is False
+        assert valid_symbol_name('a-b') is False
+
+    def test_valid_varname(self):
+        assert valid_varname('myvar') is True
+        assert valid_varname('def') is False
+
+    def test_make_symbol_table(self):
+        symtable = make_symbol_table(use_numpy=False)
+        assert 'sqrt' in symtable
+        assert 'sin' in symtable
+        symtable_custom = make_symbol_table(use_numpy=False, myvar=42)
+        assert symtable_custom['myvar'] == 42
+
+
+if __name__ == '__main__':
+    pytest.main([__file__, '-v'])
